reasoning: The question asks if Kliment Kolesnikov will hold the 50m backstroke world record on Feb 8, 2026, and March 3, 2026. The 50m backstroke world record is currently 23.55, set by Kolesnikov on July 27, 2023, and fully ratified. I checked for any very recent news of the record being broken or tied and found none. I also checked the World Aquatics calendar for February and March 2026 and found no major long course (50m) competitions that would provide a likely venue for the record to be broken during this one-month window (the schedule features artistic swimming, open water, diving, etc., and a short course championship later in the year). Kolesnikov has recently returned to international competition as a neutral athlete in 2025 and there is no indication of any doping suspension that would invalidate his 2023 record. World records in swimming generally stand for years, and the chance of one being broken or invalidated in a quiet 30-day period without major competition is extremely low. Therefore, the probability that the record list on Wikipedia will continue to show Kolesnikov on the resolution dates is very high.
